title: pavonia subhastata f. patuliloba
intro: Pavonia subhastata f. patuliloba, also known as Mexican rose or rose mallow, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Pavonia subhastata f. patuliloba include its use as an antiseptic for wound healing, a treatment for fever and cough, and a remedy for digestive issues. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared in various forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
